operating_state: Total revenue reached $254.5 billion for fiscal 2024, representing 5.0% growth driven by membership fee increases to $4.8 billion and comparable sales expansion of 5% across all segments. The company operates 890 warehouses globally after opening 30 new locations, with membership reaching 76.2 million paid members and Executive membership penetration at 73.3% of worldwide net sales. E-commerce accelerated 16% after prior year decline, while international operations generated 28% of net sales with continued strength across regions.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 35 basis points to 10.92% driven by membership fee leverage, favorable product mix shifts toward higher-margin categories, and warehouse ancillary business growth including e-commerce and gasoline operations. Operating expenses rose driven by wage increases implemented in July 2024 raising starting wages to $19.50 per hour, partially offset by operational efficiencies and scale benefits across the warehouse network.
material_events: Membership fees increased effective September 1, 2024, raising Gold Star and Business memberships to $65 annually and Executive membership to $130, expected to generate approximately $370 million in additional revenue over two years. The company paid a $15 per share special dividend totaling $6.7 billion and increased quarterly dividend 14% to $1.16 per share, while completing $698 million in share repurchases. Gary Millerchip joined as CFO in March 2024.
macro_exposures: Government tariff actions, particularly between China and the United States, continue affecting merchandise costs with exposure dependent on goods type, rates imposed, and timing of implementation. Foreign currency translation adjustments negatively impacted net sales by $474 million from Canadian and Other International operations, with the company maintaining forward foreign-exchange contracts to hedge exposures. Gasoline price deflation reduced net sales despite volume increases.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open up to 29 new warehouses in fiscal 2025 with capital expenditures expected at approximately $5.0 billion focused on warehouse construction, technology infrastructure, and supply chain optimization. The September 2024 membership fee increases are projected to benefit fiscal 2025 by $190 million, primarily in the latter half of the year. The company maintains $2.5 billion remaining under its share repurchase authorization expiring January 2027.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple legal proceedings pending including California labor law class actions, opioid litigation, privacy law violations from website pixel trackers, and False Claims Act investigation regarding prescription medications. EPA seeks administrative fines for alleged pesticide product violations, while cybersecurity threats pose increasing risks to IT systems and member data. Self-insurance liabilities of $1.6 billion rely on actuarial assumptions that could vary significantly from historical experience.
